q: The five kingdom classification was proposed by whom and in which year?
explanation: Whittaker (1969) classified into Monera, Protista, Fungi, Plantae, Animalia based on cell structure, body organisation, nutrition, reproduction & phylogeny.

q: The cell wall of fungi is composed of which substance?
explanation: Fungal cell wall = chitin (N-acetylglucosamine); same as arthropod exoskeleton. Plant=cellulose, Bacteria=peptidoglycan, Animals=no cell wall.

q: Which kingdom includes prokaryotic organisms lacking a true nucleus?
explanation: Monera = prokaryotes (no nucleus/organelles, 70S ribosomes). Includes bacteria, cyanobacteria, archaebacteria, Mycoplasma.

q: Binomial nomenclature was introduced by which scientist?
explanation: Linnaeus introduced Genus+species naming in Systema Naturae (10th ed., 1758). Father of Taxonomy.

q: The Three Domain system (Archaea, Bacteria, Eukarya) was proposed by whom and on what basis?
explanation: Woese (1990) used 16S/18S rRNA. Showed Archaea & Bacteria as distinct as each is from Eukarya.

q: Bryophytes are called the 'amphibians of the plant kingdom' because they:
options: Live both on land and in water
Need water for fertilisation & lack vascular tissue
explanation: Bryophytes (mosses) live on land but need water for motile male gametes to reach egg. No xylem/phloem; have rhizoids. First land plants.

q: Which is the LARGEST animal phylum by number of species?
explanation: Arthropoda = ~80% of animal species (>1.2 million). Jointed legs, chitin exoskeleton, moulting. Mollusca is 2nd largest.

q: Stinging cells (nematocysts) are characteristic of which phylum?
explanation: Cnidaria (hydra, jellyfish) have cnidoblasts/nematocysts for defence & prey. Diploblastic, radial symmetry, polyp/medusa forms.

q: Match: 1.Earthworm 2.Tapeworm 3.Ascaris 4.Starfish
explanation: Earthworm=Annelida (segmented), Tapeworm=Platyhelminthes (flat), Ascaris=Nematoda (round), Starfish=Echinodermata (water vascular system).

q: The Gangetic Dolphin (Platanista gangetica) belongs to which vertebrate class?
explanation: Dolphin is a mammal — warm-blooded, lungs, viviparous, mammary glands. National Aquatic Animal. Blind, uses echolocation. NOT a fish.

q: Monocots differ from dicots in which combination?
options: Monocots: 1 cotyledon, parallel venation, fibrous roots; Dicots: 2 cotyledons, reticulate, taproot
Monocots: floral parts in 4s/5s; Dicots: in 3s
explanation: Monocots=1 cotyledon, parallel venation, fibrous root, floral parts in 3s, scattered vascular bundles. Dicots=2 cotyledons, reticulate, taproot, parts in 4/5, bundles in ring.

q: Which organism is the 'connecting link' between plants and animals?
explanation: Euglena has chloroplasts (photosynthesis, plant-like) + feeds heterotrophically + lacks cell wall (animal-like). Kingdom Protista.

q: 'Amphibians of the plant kingdom' refers to which group?
explanation: Bryophytes — first land plants, need water for fertilisation, no vascular tissue, rhizoids. Mnemonic trap: Pteridophytes also need water but the specific phrase is reserved for Bryophytes.

q: Makhana (Euryale ferox), Bihar's GI-tagged aquatic crop, belongs to which family & group?
explanation: Makhana = Euryale ferox, family Nymphaeaceae, Angiosperm-Dicot. Bihar >85% of world production. 'Mithila Makhana' GI tag 2022.

q: Gymnosperms differ from angiosperms in which key feature?
options: Gymnosperms have naked seeds; angiosperms have enclosed seeds & flowers
explanation: Gymnosperms (Pinus, Cycas) = naked seeds, no flower/fruit. Angiosperms = seeds enclosed in fruit, bear flowers. Both are vascular seed plants.

q: The exoskeleton of arthropods is made of which substance?
explanation: Arthropod exoskeleton = chitin (same as fungal cell wall). Moulting/ecdysis needed to grow. Keratin is vertebrate (hair, nails).

q: Which is NOT a characteristic of phylum Echinodermata?
options: Radial symmetry in adults, bilateral in larvae; marine
explanation: Jointed appendages + chitin exoskeleton are Arthropoda features. Echinoderms (starfish) have water vascular system, tube feet, calcareous plates, radial adults, marine only.

q: Which animal has a FOUR-chambered heart?
explanation: Crocodile is the only reptile with 4-chambered heart. Birds & mammals also have 4 chambers. Sequence: Pisces(2)→Amphibia(3)→Reptilia(3,except croc=4)→Aves(4)→Mammalia(4).

q: The scientific name of the Indian Peacock (National Bird) is:
explanation: Pavo cristatus = Indian Peafowl, National Bird. Found in Bihar's Valmiki TR, Rajgir. Class Aves. Schedule I WPA 1972. Platanista = dolphin.

q: Correct taxonomic hierarchy (highest to lowest) is:
explanation: King Philip Came Over For Good Soup. Kingdom→Phylum→Class→Order→Family→Genus→Species.

q: Which is the smallest living cell and lacks a cell wall?
explanation: Mycoplasma (~0.1 μm) — smallest free-living cell, no cell wall (pleomorphic), prokaryote. Resistant to penicillin. PPLO.

q: Vikramshila Gangetic Dolphin Sanctuary (Asia's only dolphin sanctuary) is in which Bihar district?
explanation: Vikramshila Dolphin Sanctuary, Bhagalpur (1991), 50 km Ganga stretch (Sultanganj-Kahalgaon). Protects Platanista gangetica. Valmiki TR is in West Champaran (Bettiah).

q: Sal (Shorea robusta), dominant tree of Bihar's forests, is classified as:
explanation: Sal = Shorea robusta, angiosperm-dicot (family Dipterocarpaceae). Has flowers, enclosed seeds, reticulate venation. Dominant in Bihar forests (VTR, Kaimur, Bhimbandh). Deciduous timber tree.

q: Which list contains only organisms of Kingdom Protista?
explanation: Protista = unicellular eukaryotes: Amoeba, Paramecium, Euglena, Plasmodium. Others: option A=Monera, B=Fungi, D=Plantae.

q: Statements about Gangetic Dolphin: 1.National Aquatic Animal 2.Blind, uses echolocation 3.Belongs to Class Pisces 4.Schedule I WPA 1972. Which are correct?
explanation: Statements 1,2,4 correct. Statement 3 is WRONG — dolphin is Mammalia (not Pisces): lungs, viviparous, mammary glands, 4-chambered heart. National Dolphin Day = Oct 5.
